title: Vertebral column
body_html:
Spinal anatomy

Image showing normal anatomical overview of the vertebrae, intervertebral discs and exiting nerve roots



Image showing comparison of normal anatomy and that seen in disc proplapse


Image showing ligaments of vertebral column


Ligaments of the vertebral column

LigamentAttachmentsFunction
Anterior longitudinal ligament
  • Superiorly: blends to become the anterior atlanto-occipital membrane at C1 level
  • During course: attaches to anterior of vertebral bodies and annulus
  • Inferiorly: anterosuperior portion of the sacrum
  • Prevent hyperextension (limits extension) of the vertebral column
  • Support intervertebral disc
    • Posteriorlongitudinal ligament
    • Superiorly: tectorial membrane (atlanto-axial joint and occiput)
    • During course: attaches to posterior of vertebral bodies and annulus
    • Inferiorly: sacrum
  • Prevent hyperflexion (limits flexion) of the vertebral column
  • Support intervertebral disc
    • Ligamenta flava
    • Attach pedicles of adjacent vertebrae
    • Extends from axis (C2) to sacrum (S1)
  • Preserve upright posture
  • Elastic recoil after flexion
    • Supraspinous ligament
    • Connects tips of spinous processes from C7 to the sacrum
  • Limit hyperflexion of the vertebral column
    • Interspinous ligament
    • Connect adjoining spinous processes
    • Extend from the root to the apex of each spinous process
    • Positioned between ligamenta flava and supraspinous ligament
  • Limit flexion of the spine
    • Intertransverse ligament
    • Between the transverse processes of the spine
  • Limit lateral flexion of the spine

    • Illustration of spinal ligaments

    4
